title: A method and a system for disabling a motor vehicle and a monitoring system for monitoring a motor vehicle
abstract: A control system (1) for disabling a motor vehicle (2) comprising a controller (3) located in the motor vehicle (2) for operating an immobiliser (5) of a vehicle security system of the motor vehicle (2). A vehicle wireless receiver (14) is located in the vehicle (2) for receiving a disable signal from a primary transmitter (19) of a building security system (15) when the building security system (15) is armed. The controller (3) reads signals received by the vehicle receiver (14), and on receiving the disable signal transmitted by the primary transmitter (19) of the building security system (15), the controller (3) operates the immobiliser (5) into the disabled mode, for in turn disabling the motor vehicle (2). A monitoring system is also provided for monitoring a motor vehicle in a parking area, and comprises an image capturing device, which captures images of the parking area for detecting a person in close proximity to the motor vehicle.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method for disabling a motor vehicle comprising wirelessly transmitting a disable signal from a building security system of a house or building, configuring a vehicle wireless receiver located in the motor vehicle to receive the disable signal, and configuring a control means located in the motor vehicle to read signals from the vehicle wireless receiver and to operate a vehicle security system located in the motor vehicle to disable the motor vehicle in response to reception by the vehicle wireless receiver of the disable signal transmitted from the building security system. 2. A method as claimed in Claim 1 in which the control means is configured to maintain the motor vehicle disabled until an override signal is received by the vehicle wireless receiver or is inputted through a vehicle input means of the motor vehicle to the control means. 3. A method as claimed in Claim 1 or 2 in which the control means is configured to receive the override signal as one of an override code inputted to the control means through the vehicle input means, a code generated by simultaneous operation of a plurality of predefined operating switches of the motor vehicle, a code generated by operating a plurality of predefined operating switches of the motor vehicle in a predefined sequence, and a signal indicative of one of a finger, thumb or handprint, an image of an eye or a part thereof or a facial image of an authorised person. 4. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the vehicle input means is configured to capture the signal indicative of the finger, thumb or handprint or the image of an eye or a part thereof or the facial image of the authorised person. 5. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the vehicle input means comprises one of a vehicle keypad, a vehicle touch screen configured as a keypad, and an image capturing device. 6. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the building security system is provided with a primary wireless transmitter configured to transmit the disable signal from the building security system within a predefined limited range. 7. A method as claimed in Claim 6 in which the predefined limited range is less than 200 metres. 8. A method as claimed in Claim 6 or 7 in which the predefined limited range is less than 150 metres. 9. A method as claimed in any of Claims 6 to 8 in which the predefined limited range lies in the range of 1 metre to 100 metres. 10. A method as claimed in any of Claims 6 to 9 in which the primary wireless transmitter is configured to transmit the disable signal from the building security system in a Bluetooth protocol. 11. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is configured in response to reception of the disable signal from the building security system to carry out one or more of the following functions, lock all the doors of the motor vehicle, close any open windows of the motor vehicle, close a sunroof of the motor vehicle, and lock the boot lid of the motor vehicle. 12. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the disable signal produced by the building security system comprises a disable signal which is similar to or different from a normal disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 13. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the disable signal produced by the building security system is different to a normal disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 14. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is configured to be responsive to a normal disable signal and a normal enable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 15. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the normal disable signal comprises a normal disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter located in a key fob transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 16. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the normal enable signal comprises a normal enable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter located in a key fob transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 17. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is responsive to the normal enable signal for enabling the motor vehicle to operate in a normal street mode. 18. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is responsive to the normal disable signal for disabling the motor vehicle. 19. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which when the motor vehicle is enabled in the normal street mode, the control means is configured to be unresponsive to the disable signal produced by the building security system until the ignition system of the motor vehicle has been deactivated. 20. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which when the motor vehicle is enabled in the normal street mode, the control means is configured to be unresponsive to the disable signal produced by the building security system until the driver and the passengers have left the motor vehicle. 21. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means comprises a controller. 22. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means comprises a signal processor. 23. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is provided by a controller of the vehicle security system. 24. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the vehicle wireless receiver is provided by a wireless receiver of the vehicle security system of the vehicle. 25. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which a vehicle wireless transmitter is located in the motor vehicle, and is configured to transmit a signal for reception by the building security system. 26. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which a primary wireless receiver is located in or adjacent the building security system communicating with the building security system for receiving a signal transmitted by the vehicle wireless transmitter. 27. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the control means is responsive to reception from the vehicle wireless receiver of the disable signal from the primary transmitter to produce a 

vehicle disable signal for disabling the motor vehicle. 28. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the vehicle security system is responsive to the vehicle disable signal produced by the control means for disabling the motor vehicle. 29. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which a CAN module is located in the motor vehicle to receive from the vehicle wireless receiver the disable signal transmitted from the primary transmitter. 30. A method as claimed in Claim 29 in which the CAN module comprises an analogue communications module. 31. A method as claimed in Claim 29 in which the CAN module comprises a digital communications module. 32. A method as claimed in Claim 29 in which the CAN module comprises both an analogue and a digital communications module. 33. A method as claimed in any preceding claim in which the vehicle security system is configured to carry out one or more of the following functions: disable the ignition system of the motor vehicle, isolate the engine of the motor vehicle from the fuel tank of the motor vehicle, and isolate the ignition system of the motor vehicle from the battery thereof. 34. A control system for disabling a motor vehicle, the control system comprising a primary wireless 

transmitter for transmitting a disable signal from a building security system of a house or building, a vehicle wireless receiver located in the motor vehicle configured for receiving the disable signal transmitted by the primary wireless transmitter of the building security system, and a first control means located in the motor vehicle and configured to read signals received by the vehicle wireless receiver, the first control means being configured to operate a vehicle security system located in the motor vehicle to disable the motor vehicle in response to reception by the wireless receiver of the disable signal transmitted from the building security system. 35. A control system as claimed in Claim 34 in which the control means is configured to maintain the motor vehicle disabled until an override signal is received by the vehicle wireless receiver or is inputted through a vehicle input means of the motor vehicle to the control means. 36. A control system as claimed in Claim 34 or 35 in which the control means is configured to receive the override signal as one of an override code inputted to the control means through the vehicle input means, a code generated by simultaneous operation of a plurality of predefined operating switches of the motor vehicle, a code generated by operating a plurality of predefined operating switches of the motor vehicle in a predefined sequence, and a signal indicative of one of a finger, thumb or handprint, an image of an eye or a part thereof or a facial image of an authorised person. 37. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 36 in which the vehicle input means is configured to capture the signal indicative of the finger, thumb or handprint or the image of an eye ora part thereof or the facial image of the authorised person. 38. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 37 in which the vehicle input means comprises one of a vehicle keypad, a vehicle touch screen configured as a keypad, and an image 

capturing device. 39. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 38 in which the building security system is provided with a primary wireless transmitter configured to transmit the disable signal from the building security system within a predefined limited range. 40. A control system as claimed in Claim 39 in which the predefined limited range is less than 200 metres. 41. A control system as claimed in Claim 39 or 40 in which the predefined limited range is less than 150 metres. 42. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 39 to 41 in which the predefined limited range lies in the range of 1 metre to 100 metres. 43. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 42 in which the primary wireless transmitter is configured to transmit the disable signal from the building security system in a Bluetooth protocol. 44. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 43 in which the control means is configured in response to reception of the disable signal from the building security system to carry out one or more of the following functions, lock all the car doors of the motor vehicle, close any open windows of the motor vehicle, close a sunroof of the motor vehicle, and lock the boot lid of the motor vehicle. 45. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 44 in which the disable signal produced by the building security system comprises a disable signal which is similar to or different from a normal 

disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 46. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 45 in which the disable signal produced by the building security system is different to the normal disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 47. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 46 in which the control means is configured to be responsive to a normal disable signal and a normal enable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 48. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 47 in which the normal disable signal comprises a normal disable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter located in a key fob transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 49. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 48 in which the normal enable signal comprises a normal enable signal transmitted by a remote wireless transmitter located in a key fob transmitter associated with the vehicle security system. 50. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 49 in which the control means is responsive to the normal enable signal for enabling the motor vehicle to operate in a normal street mode. 51. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 50 in which the control means is responsive to the normal disable signal for disabling the motor vehicle. 52. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 51 in which when the motor vehicle is 

enabled in the normal street mode, the control means is configured to be unresponsive to the disable signal produced by the building security system until the ignition system of the motor vehicle has been deactivated. 53. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 52 in which when the motor vehicle is enabled in the normal street mode, the control means is configured to be unresponsive to the disable signal produced by the building security system until the driver and the passengers have left the motor vehicle. 54. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 53 in which the control means comprises a controller. 55. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 54 in which the control means comprises a signal processor. 56. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 55 in which the control means is provided by a controller of the vehicle security system. 57. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 56 in which the vehicle wireless receiver is provided by a wireless receiver of the vehicle security system of the vehicle. 58. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 57 in which a vehicle wireless transmitter is located in the motor vehicle, and is configured to transmit a signal for reception by the building security system. 59. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 58 in which a primary wireless receiver is located in or adjacent the building security system communicating with the building security system for receiving a signal transmitted by the vehicle wireless transmitter. 60. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 59 in which the control means is responsive to reception from the vehicle wireless receiver of the disable signal from the primary transmitter to produce a vehicle disable signal for disabling the motor vehicle. 61. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 60 in which the vehicle security system is responsive to the vehicle disable signal produced by the control means for disabling the motor vehicle. 62. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 61 in which a CAN module is located in the motor vehicle to receive from the vehicle wireless receiver the disable signal transmitted from the primary transmitter. 63. A control system as claimed in Claim 62 in which the CAN module comprises an analogue communications module. 64. A control system as claimed in Claim 62 in which the CAN module comprises a digital communications module. 65. A control system as claimed in Claim 62 in which the CAN module comprises both an analogue and a digital communications module. 66. A control system as claimed in any of Claims 34 to 65 in which the vehicle security system is 

configured to carry out one or more of the following functions: disable the ignition system of the motor vehicle, isolate the engine of the motor vehicle from the fuel tank of the motor vehicle, and isolate the ignition system of the motor vehicle from the battery thereof. 67. A monitoring system for monitoring a motor vehicle in a parking area, the monitoring system comprising an image capturing device for capturing an image of the parking area, a signal processor for reading signals from the image capturing device indicative of an image captured by the image capturing device, the signal processor being configured to analyse the signals indicative of the captured image and to identify the presence of a person in the parking area in proximity to the motor vehicle, a transmitter operated under the control of the signal processor and configured to wirelessly transmit an alert signal to at least one remote receiver in response to the signal processor identifying the presence of a person in proximity to the motor vehicle. 68. A monitoring system as claimed in Claim 67 in which the transmitter is configured to transmit signals indicative of the captured image to the at least one remote receiver in response to the signal processor identifying the presence of a person in proximity to the motor vehicle. 69. A monitoring system as claimed in Claim 67 or 68 in which the transmitter is configured to transmit the one or both of the alert signal and the signals indicative of the captured image to one of the remote receivers located in a central monitoring station. 70. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 69 in which the transmitter is configured to transmit the one or both of the alert signal and the signals indicative of the captured image to one of the remote receivers comprising a mobile phone. 71. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 70 in which the image capturing device is configured to capture a video image. 72. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 71 in which the image capturing device comprises a charge coupled device. 73. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 72 in which the signal processor is configured to continuously read signals from the image capturing device and to continuously analyse the signals read from the image capturing device. 74. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 73 in which the transmitter comprises a wireless transmitter. 75. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 74 in which the transmitter is configured to communicate with the at least one remote receiver via the internet. 76. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 75 in which the image capturing device is located in or on the motor vehicle. 77. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 76 in which the signal processor is located in the motor vehicle. 78. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 77 in which the transmitter is located in or on the motor vehicle. 79. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 78 in which the monitoring system is configured to communicate with a building security system. 80. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 79 in which the monitoring system is configured to communicate wirelessly with the at least one remote receiver through the building security system. 81. A monitoring system as claimed in any of Claims 67 to 80 in which a vehicle wireless receiver is provided in or on the motor vehicle for receiving a signal transmitted in response to the at least one remote receiver having received the alert signal, the signal transmitted in response to the at least one remote receiver receiving the alert signal comprising a signal indicative of a spoken message containing words to advise a person in proximity to the motor vehicle to leave the parking area or a signal configured to activate the signal processor to produce such a signal indicative of such a spoken message, and a speaker is provided in or on the motor vehicle operable under the control of the signal processor to and configured to broadcast the spoken message.
